note: My Health Records Act 2012 s.77(1), read with its full (a)-(c) prohibitions, bars holding, taking, processing or handling My Health Record system records/information outside Australia. It is a single narrow health-record localization measure, so 0.5; the corpus absence row missed this P6-I1 mapping. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

Section 77 prohibits offshore holding/processing; it does not permit offshore use while requiring a domestic copy. Under the P6-I2 local-copy test this is P6-I1 evidence, not a local-copy requirement. No separate qualifying P6-I2 rule was established. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

No reviewed measure makes a local server, data centre or facility a condition of providing a service. Storage/processing restrictions and regulation of existing infrastructure do not satisfy P6-I3. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

Privacy Act 1988 Schedule 1, APP 8.1 requires an APP entity, before overseas disclosure, to take reasonable steps to ensure the recipient does not breach the APPs; APP 8.2 supplies exceptions. This is a horizontal conditional cross-border flow regime. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

Australia-Singapore Digital Economy Agreement Article 23 contains a binding cross-border transfer obligation and entered into force on 8 Dec 2020; Australia is also a CPTPP party. At least one binding agreement exists, therefore inverse-polarity P6-I5 = 0. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

The Privacy Act 1988 and its Australian Privacy Principles provide a horizontal personal-information framework, including collection/use/disclosure, security, access and correction principles. The absence row reflects corpus acquisition failure, not absence of law. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

The in-force Cyber Security Act 2024 is a dedicated national cybersecurity statute covering smart-device standards, ransomware reporting, cyber-incident coordination/review and enforcement. This newer horizontal framework supports 0; the older master 0.5 predates it. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

Multiple complete provisions specify minimum periods: AML/CTF Act ss.111(1), 114A(1) and 117(2) require seven years; My Health Records Act s.17(2) also supplies a long fixed period. One qualifying minimum is sufficient. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

Privacy Act s.33D lets the Commissioner direct a particular agency to provide a PIA when statutory conditions are met. It is discretionary and agency-only, not an all-sector or standing sector-wide DPO/DPIA requirement; P7-I4 therefore remains 0. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

At least one reviewed power does not require independent judicial authorization: Surveillance Devices Act 2004 s.39(1) permits tracking-device use without warrant with administrative permission, and TIA Act s.178 uses authorised-officer approval. Warrant-gated ASIO s.25A does not negate those powers. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

Act 709 s.129, read as a whole, creates conditional transfer paths (specified places, adequacy, consent, contract, legal rights and other exceptions). It is P6-I4, not an absolute transfer ban or mandatory domestic-processing rule. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

Two distinct domestic-storage duties qualify: Service Tax Act 2018 s.24 requires covered records to be kept in Malaysia unless the Director General approves otherwise; Income Tax Act 1967 s.82 requires Malaysian business records to be retained in Malaysia. Two specific-data rules aggregate to 1 under P6-I2. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

No reviewed law requires a local server, data centre or facility as a precondition to provide the service. Conditional transfer, security controls and licensing do not satisfy P6-I3. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

Act 709 s.129 was reviewed through subsections (1)-(6): overseas personal-data transfer is conditioned on a specified/adequate destination or enumerated grounds such as consent, contract, legal rights, due diligence, vital interests or public interest. Horizontal personal-data coverage gives 1. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

CPTPP Article 14.11 requires Parties to allow cross-border electronic transfer for covered persons, subject to its public-policy exception. CPTPP entered into force for Malaysia on 29 Nov 2022, so at least one binding agreement exists and P6-I5 = 0. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

Personal Data Protection Act 2010 is a horizontal commercial personal-data framework and the enacted A1727 amendments add breach notification, DPO and other protections. The selected amendment snippets are not the whole framework; legal existence and core rights support 0. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

Cyber Security Act 2024 (Act 854), read with its NCII duties, incident notification, risk assessment/audit and cybersecurity-service licensing provisions, is a dedicated national cybersecurity framework. The rubric expressly treats Act 854 as score 0. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

Companies Act 2016 s.341 and the reviewed amendment provisions impose a seven-year minimum on company records. A clearly specified minimum period is sufficient for P7-I3 = 1. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

Enacted Act A1727 s.12A requires data controllers and processors to appoint one or more data protection officers; the official commencement instrument brought the DPO provisions into operation and Commissioner Circular 1/2025 applies from 1 Jun 2025. Horizontal requirement gives 1. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

Security Offences (Special Measures) Act 2012 s.6(3), read with ss.6(1)-(7), lets a superintendent-level police officer intercept communications in urgent cases without Public Prosecutor authorization and without independent judicial authorization. This independently satisfies P7-I5 = 1. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

PDPA s.26 permits overseas transfer when prescribed comparable-protection requirements are met. This is a conditional-flow regime, not an absolute ban or requirement to process domestically; no other qualifying P6-I1 measure was established. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

Companies Act 1967 s.199(3)-(4), read in full, requires that where accounting records are outside Singapore, sufficient statements and returns be sent to and kept in Singapore. This is one specific accounting-data domestic-copy rule, giving 0.5. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

No reviewed measure requires a local server, data centre or other facility as a condition of providing a service. PDPA transfer safeguards and cybersecurity controls do not meet P6-I3. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

PDPA s.26 and Personal Data Protection Regulations 2021 regs.10 and 12 impose comparable-protection, consent and legally enforceable-obligation conditions on overseas personal-data transfers. Horizontal personal-data conditional flow gives 1. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

DEPA Article 4.3 and the Singapore-Australia Digital Economy Agreement Article 23 each contain binding cross-border electronic-transfer obligations. Their public-policy exceptions do not remove the binding commitment; P6-I5 = 0. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

The PDPA is a horizontal framework governing collection, use, disclosure, care, access and correction of personal data, administered by the PDPC. Reading only the three retrieved snippets understates the complete statute; the framework is comprehensive enough for 0. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

Cybersecurity Act 2018 is a dedicated national cybersecurity framework covering CII, incident response, information sharing, regulatory oversight and cybersecurity-service providers, expanded by the 2024 amendments. The rubric expressly gives Singapore's Act score 0. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

Companies Act s.199(2) requires at least five years, Income Tax Act s.67 requires five years, and additional sectoral provisions specify minimum periods. These are minimum retention duties, not PDPA retention ceilings, so P7-I3 = 1. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

PDPA s.11 was reviewed through subsections (1)-(6); s.11(3) mandates that every organisation designate one or more individuals responsible for PDPA compliance, with public contact details under s.11(5). All-sector DPO-equivalent requirement gives 1. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]

Criminal Procedure Code s.34(1) expressly permits a police search without a search warrant when its conditions hold, and s.39 permits investigating police/authorised persons to access and inspect computers without prior independent judicial authorization. One such power gives 1. [AI suggestion ratified as final by reviewer instruction, 20 Jul final session.]
